description_long: This house features a steeply pitched red gable roof and a two-story structure with timber-framed detailing, reminiscent of traditional European or Tudor-style homes. The front facade displays a prominent balcony with wooden railings and a set of illuminated windows, suggesting a warm, inviting interior. The exterior walls are finished in a light beige color, accented by dark brown wooden beams and trim. On one side, a covered staircase leads up to the second floor, providing access to the upper level and adding architectural interest. The windows are arranged in a combination of horizontal and vertical groupings, with some featuring decorative grilles. The overall design is compact yet detailed, with a clear emphasis on symmetry and classic residential aesthetics.
description: A two-story house with a steep red gable roof, timber-framed beige walls, a front balcony, and a side staircase, evoking a traditional European style.
description_view_0: Front view showing the balcony, illuminated windows, and timber-framed facade.
description_view_1: Rear view dominated by the large red gable roof and plain beige wall with minimal detailing.
description_view_2: Side view highlighting the depth of the roof and the solid wall with visible timber trim.
description_view_3: Side view featuring the covered staircase leading to the upper floor and the extended roof overhang.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical placement with grouped and individual windows
attribute_entryway_design: Main entryway accessed via a covered staircase, with a small porch and wooden railing, no columns or elaborate decorative elements.
